name: code-tier-guard
description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block enforcing commons @D g78 (code-tier — edit a remote repo over its sshfs mount, never a local clone), implemented in hexa-lang (`_code_tier_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads ~/.sidecar/codetier.conf (`name|host:remote_dir|mountpoint|localpath`); for any tier that declares a 4th `localpath` field, a Write/Edit/NotebookEdit or Bash write channel (`>`, `>>`, tee, dd of=, cp/mv dest; relative targets resolved against payload cwd) whose target resolves UNDER that local clone is denied with an action-first redirect to the same relative path under the tier's MOUNT. SAFETY RAILS (skip-on-doubt, never over-block): `.git/` under the local clone is exempt (local git stays usable) · the mountpoint itself (and anything under it) is exempt (the correct edit path) · a target not under any registered localpath passes · a tier whose localpath equals its mountpoint is ignored · absent config or no localpath fields → inert (allows everything). The gate is a real precondition (a registered localpath + a target under it), not an opt-out switch (project.tape @D s11); enforcement is the hard deny (s13). Pairs with the `sidecar mount` CLI (the launcher that registers tiers + the localpath field). NO env-var / flag override by design.
